channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and flips trucks as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday overturned semitrucks on highways and fanned wildfires in Texas, Oklahoma, Missouri and Kansas, prompting evacuation warnings in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Federal judge considers blocking DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge is considering whether to temporarily block Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Social Security Administration systems that hold sensitive data on millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USPS agrees to work with DOGE on reform, planning to cut 10,000 workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Postmaster General Louis DeJoy plans to cut 10,000 workers and billions of dollars from the U.S. Postal Service budget and he’ll do that working with Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency, according to a letter sent to members of Congress on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Arrest made after crash kills at least 5 people, including infant, in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, KRON4

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Former reality TV star Jessie Holmes wins longest-ever Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race in Alaska
item.description: Former reality TV star Jessie Holmes has won the longest-ever Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race in Alaska.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: President Donald Trump used a visit to the Justice Department on Friday to air a litany of grievances about the criminal investigations that threatened to torpedo his political career.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Lawsuit accuses man of killing, eating patients at mental health facility
item.description: The case against a South Carolina mental health facility where a man is accused of murdering and cannibalizing patients could be heading to trial.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE Boston arrests Guatemalan national charged with child rape who was released back into community by court
item.description: Guatemalan national, Stivenson Omar Perez-Ajtzalan, 19, was arrested on Jan. 30 for aggravated rape of a child 10 years younger than him, according to ICE Boston.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Founding girl group member reveals she's been living in her car
item.description: "If you would have said to me while I was En Vogue, 'You're going to be living in your car one day,' I'd be like, 'Huh?"
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: This week, Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells. The recalled glass water bottles are being recalled over concerns they can crack, causing risk of cuts or slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Schumer says he will vote to advance GOP spending bill, lowering threat of shutdown
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) announced on the Senate floor Thursday that he plans to vote to advance a House-passed six-month government funding bill, despite the strong opposition of many Democrats who say the bill would create a “slush fund” for President Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US: Where, when it will hit
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. will be in the path of an intense March storm starting Friday as the sprawling multi-day system threatens fires, blizzards, tornadoes, and flooding as it tracks eastward across the Great Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: March megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. will be in the path of an intense March storm starting Friday as the sprawling multi-day system threatens fires, blizzards, tornadoes, and flooding as it tracks eastward across the Great Plains.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Lawsuit claims Girl Scout cookies contain 'heavy metals,' pesticides
item.description: A new federal lawsuit claims Girl Scout Cookies contain heavy metals, but the organization assures the cookies are safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KRON4

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, WGN 9

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: 56 percent disapprove of Trump handling of economy: Survey
item.description: The survey comes as Trump faces criticism for his tariff policies and as the stock market has seen significant dips amid concerns of a recession.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina House Republicans fight with each other over the budget
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — At least three-quarters of South Carolina's House members approved the more than 100 sections of the state's $14 billion spending plan this week, but is wasn't as easy as that makes it seem.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 men accused of blocking traffic with twerking
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (WREG) -- Five people were arrested Monday night after police say they blocked a downtown intersection with their dancing.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5
